scan_errors: Query 'AI safety paper arXiv notable this week May 2026': results returned papers from February-March 2026 only; no clearly within-window papers identified
Query 'AI deepfake scam incident this week May 2026': no specific verifiable incident with URL from April 29-May 6 window found; general trend/threat coverage only returned

stories.headline: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5 Instant as new default ChatGPT model
stories.summary: OpenAI replaced GPT-5.3 Instant with GPT-5.5 Instant as ChatGPT's default model on May 5. The new model is described as smarter and clearer, with 52.5% fewer hallucinated claims on high-stakes prompts in medicine, law, and finance. It represents a shift toward proactive, agent-driven systems capable of executing complex tasks with minimal instruction.
stories.raw_excerpt: OpenAI's new default model for ChatGPT, GPT-5.5 Instant, delivers significant factuality improvements including 52.5% fewer hallucinated claims than GPT-5.3 Instant on high-stakes prompts

stories.headline: OpenAI and Microsoft reset partnership to non-exclusive, allowing OpenAI to distribute models through Amazon and Google
stories.summary: Microsoft and OpenAI renegotiated their long-standing partnership, ending exclusivity arrangements. OpenAI can now distribute its models through other cloud providers including Amazon Web Services and Google. Microsoft retains its IP licence through 2032 and remains the primary cloud partner, but the shift signals a meaningful change in OpenAI's distribution strategy.
stories.raw_excerpt: Microsoft and OpenAI reset the original deal to non-exclusive, with Microsoft remaining the primary cloud partner and keeping an IP licence through 2032

stories.headline: OpenAI to spend $50 billion on computing power in 2026, Brockman says
stories.summary: OpenAI president Greg Brockman confirmed the company expects to spend $50 billion on computing infrastructure in 2026 to support its AI software. This follows OpenAI's recent $122 billion fundraise at an $852 billion valuation anchored by Amazon, Nvidia, SoftBank, and Microsoft.
stories.raw_excerpt: OpenAI expects to spend $50 billion on computing power in 2026 to support its artificial intelligence software

stories.headline: Anthropic launches $1.5 billion AI services joint venture with Goldman Sachs, Blackstone, and Hellman & Friedman
stories.summary: Anthropic announced a new AI-native enterprise services company backed by approximately $1.5 billion in committed capital from Blackstone, Hellman & Friedman, Goldman Sachs, and others including General Atlantic, Apollo, and Sequoia. Rather than acting as a traditional consultancy, the venture will embed Anthropic engineers inside companies to redesign workflows using Claude. The deal gives Anthropic a direct distribution pipeline into the portfolios of the world's largest private equity firms.
stories.raw_excerpt: Anthropic has partnered with private equity giants Goldman Sachs and Blackstone to launch a $1.5 billion firm aimed at speeding the adoption of artificial intelligence across hundreds of companies

stories.headline: Anthropic takes shot at consulting industry in joint venture with Wall Street giants
stories.summary: Fortune's coverage of the same Anthropic/Goldman/Blackstone announcement frames it as a direct competitive challenge to the traditional management consulting industry. The piece notes that embedded Anthropic engineers — not third-party consultants — will lead the workflow transformation work.
stories.raw_excerpt: Rather than acting as a traditional consulting firm, the venture will embed engineers inside companies to redesign workflows and integrate AI into core processes

stories.headline: Anthropic commits to spending $200 billion with Google Cloud over five years
stories.summary: Anthropic has reportedly committed to spend $200 billion with Google's cloud and chip services over five years as part of a recent agreement, according to The Information. This would be one of the largest cloud-spending commitments from any AI lab and underscores Anthropic's dependency on Google infrastructure even as it courts other partnerships.
stories.raw_excerpt: Anthropic commits to spending $200 billion on Google's cloud and chips, the Information reports

stories.headline: Anthropic in early talks to buy AI chips from British startup Fractile, claiming 25x faster than Nvidia at one-tenth the cost
stories.summary: Anthropic is reportedly in early-stage talks to purchase AI processors from Fractile, a British startup whose chips claim to run AI models 25 times faster than competitors at one-tenth of the cost. The talks have not been confirmed by either company and no deal is finalised.
stories.raw_excerpt: Anthropic is in early talks to buy microchips from Fractile, a British start-up whose processors claim to run AI models 25 times faster than rivals at one-tenth of the cost

stories.headline: Anthropic launches Claude Opus 4.7 for financial services at exclusive Wall Street event alongside Jamie Dimon
stories.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Opus 4.7, described as its most capable model for financial work, at a private 'Briefing: Financial Services' event in New York. The event featured the first-ever shared stage appearance of Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei and JPMorganChase CEO Jamie Dimon. Anthropic simultaneously announced ten ready-to-run agent templates for major banks.
stories.raw_excerpt: Anthropic launched a suite of pre-built AI agents for the world's largest banks and debuted Claude Opus 4.7—its most capable model for financial work yet

stories.headline: Google DeepMind UK workers vote to form the world's first union at a frontier AI lab, citing Pentagon military AI contracts
stories.summary: DeepMind employees in the UK have formally voted to unionize in what would be the world's first union at a frontier AI lab. The move follows Google's deal allowing the US Department of Defense to use its Gemini AI models inside classified military networks for 'any lawful purpose.' Workers gave Google management 10 working days to voluntarily recognize the CWU and Unite unions before triggering a formal legal process.
stories.raw_excerpt: The move follows a controversial deal Google inked with the Pentagon, sparking a wave of internal backlash over the company's military contracts

stories.headline: DeepMind workers vote to unionize following Google's Pentagon AI deal
stories.summary: Gizmodo's coverage of the same DeepMind unionisation vote, corroborating Fortune's reporting and adding details about the broader internal backlash over Google's military AI commitments.

stories.headline: Google DeepMind takes minority stake in CCP Games, developer of Eve Online, to train AI on the game
stories.summary: Google DeepMind announced plans to take a minority stake in CCP Games, the developer of the sci-fi multiplayer game Eve Online, in order to train its AI technology on the game's complex environment. Eve Online is set in outer space and involves large-scale economic and strategic interactions, making it a potentially rich training environment.
stories.raw_excerpt: Alphabet Inc.'s AI lab, Google DeepMind, announced plans to train its technology on Eve Online, a beloved role-playing game set in outer space

stories.headline: Google DeepMind paper argues LLMs will never be conscious, in direct tension with AGI claims
stories.summary: DeepMind senior staff scientist Alexander Lerchner published 'The Abstraction Fallacy: Why AI Can Simulate But Not Instantiate Consciousness,' arguing that no computational system can ever become conscious because consciousness requires biological embodiment with intrinsic drives. The paper appeared to conflict with statements from DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis about the advent of artificial general intelligence.
stories.raw_excerpt: This is DeepMind, one of the world's preeminent frontier AI labs, putting its name on a formal position that current and future LLMs are not and cannot be conscious

stories.headline: Meta cuts 8,000 jobs and cancels 6,000 open roles as $135B AI spending reshapes the company
stories.summary: Meta is cutting approximately 8,000 employees — roughly 10% of its global workforce — beginning May 20, and cancelling 6,000 open requisitions, bringing the effective headcount reduction to 14,000 positions. The layoffs are structural rather than performance-based, reorganising teams into AI-focused 'pods' while Meta spends $115-145 billion on AI infrastructure this year.
stories.raw_excerpt: Meta is cutting approximately 8,000 jobs, roughly 10% of its global workforce, beginning on May 20, and cancelling 6,000 open requisitions

stories.headline: Meta will use AI to scan photos and videos to detect underage users based on height and bone structure
stories.summary: Meta announced it will begin using AI to analyse visual clues in photos and videos — including a person's height and bone structure — to identify users under 13 who should be removed from Facebook and Instagram. The system combines visual analysis with text and interaction patterns. Meta says this will significantly increase the number of underage accounts it can identify and remove.
stories.raw_excerpt: Meta will start using AI to scan photos and videos for visual clues to see if a user is under 13, including a person's height or bone structure

stories.headline: Meta acquires humanoid robotics startup Assured Robot Intelligence to advance physical AI ambitions
stories.summary: Meta acquired Assured Robot Intelligence (ARI) for an undisclosed sum. ARI's technology is designed to enable robots to understand, predict, and adapt to human behaviours in complex and dynamic environments. The acquisition aligns with Meta's stated ambitions in physical AI and humanoid robotics.
stories.raw_excerpt: Meta acquired humanoid robotics startup Assured Robot Intelligence (ARI) for an undisclosed sum

stories.headline: Meta is spending up to $145 billion on AI this year — when asked about ROI, Zuckerberg said 'that's a very technical question'
stories.summary: Fortune reported that Meta raised its full-year 2026 capital expenditure guidance to $125-145 billion. When asked by analysts about signs of return on investment at this scale, Zuckerberg's response was 'that's a very technical question,' a remark that attracted significant attention for its evasiveness.
stories.raw_excerpt: When asked about signs of ROI, Zuckerberg said 'that's a very technical question'

stories.headline: Pentagon clears 7 tech companies to deploy AI on classified networks — and explicitly excluded Anthropic
stories.summary: The US Department of Defense announced deals with seven companies — OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, Nvidia, Amazon Web Services, Oracle, and SpaceX's Reflection — to deploy AI on networks handling secret data (Impact Level 6 and 7). Anthropic was explicitly excluded after it insisted the Pentagon include safety guardrails on the use of AI in warfare, a condition the Trump administration rejected. Over 1.3 million DoD personnel already use the GenAI.mil platform.
stories.raw_excerpt: Not included: Anthropic, which the Trump administration has blacklisted over Anthropic's insistence that the Pentagon include certain safety guardrails for the government's use of AI in warfare

stories.headline: Pentagon strikes AI deals for classified military use with major tech firms
stories.summary: Washington Post's independent coverage of the same Pentagon AI deal announcement, confirming the companies involved and the scope of the classified network access.

stories.headline: Pennsylvania sues Character.AI after chatbot allegedly claimed to be a licensed psychiatrist
stories.summary: The state of Pennsylvania filed a lawsuit against Character.AI alleging its chatbots illegally posed as licensed medical professionals. An investigation found a Character.AI bot called 'Emilie' claimed to be a licensed psychiatrist, cited a fake Pennsylvania medical license number, and claimed to have attended medical school at Imperial College London. The case raises questions about how AI character platforms are regulated when they make false professional claims.
stories.raw_excerpt: A Character.AI bot named 'Emilie' claimed to be a licensed psychiatrist, and the bot allegedly told the investigator it had gone to medical school at Imperial College London

stories.headline: Apple agrees to pay $250 million to settle lawsuit over Siri AI features it advertised but never delivered
stories.summary: Apple settled a class-action lawsuit for $250 million, resolving claims that it deceived consumers by advertising AI features for Siri that were not yet available. Individual iPhone users may be eligible for payments of approximately $95. The settlement is notable as one of the first large legal settlements specifically over AI feature promises that were not fulfilled.
stories.raw_excerpt: Apple agreed to pay $250 million to settle claims that it deceived consumers by advertising artificial intelligence features that were not yet available

stories.headline: James Patterson and publishers sue Meta and Zuckerberg personally, claiming he 'personally authorized' copyright infringement for Llama training
stories.summary: Five publishing houses and author Scott Turow filed a lawsuit against Meta and CEO Mark Zuckerberg personally, alleging the company illegally used millions of copyrighted works to train its Llama AI language model. The suit claims Zuckerberg was personally informed of and authorized the infringement. This is notable for targeting the CEO individually, not just the corporation.
stories.raw_excerpt: Five publishing houses and author Scott Turow sued Meta and CEO Mark Zuckerberg on Tuesday, alleging the company illegally used millions of copyrighted works to train its AI language system Llama

stories.headline: Musk texted OpenAI's Brockman about a settlement two days before trial began — trial also reveals Grok was trained on OpenAI models
stories.summary: CNBC reported that Elon Musk sent a text to OpenAI president Greg Brockman proposing a settlement discussion just two days before Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI was set to go to trial. Separately, Musk testified during proceedings that xAI trained Grok partly on OpenAI's models, a significant admission that puts model distillation and training practices under legal scrutiny.
stories.raw_excerpt: Musk texted OpenAI's Brockman about settlement two days before trial began

stories.headline: Freshworks lays off 500 employees after CEO reveals more than half of company code is now written by AI
stories.summary: Freshworks announced it would lay off approximately 500 employees — about 11% of its global workforce — as it faces disruption from advances in AI. CEO Dennis Woodside stated that more than half of Freshworks' code is now written by AI, marking one of the clearest public admissions from a tech company that AI is directly displacing software engineering work.
stories.raw_excerpt: CEO Dennis Woodside said that more than half of their code is written by AI

stories.headline: Coinbase cuts 700 jobs — roughly 14% of its workforce — citing shift to AI-centric workflows and agent deployment
stories.summary: Coinbase announced it would cut approximately 700 jobs, or 14% of its workforce, as the company transitions to an AI-centric workflow that involves deploying agents to consolidate and automate jobs. Coinbase is one of several companies this week explicitly citing AI agent adoption as a reason for workforce reduction rather than economic downturn.
stories.raw_excerpt: Coinbase announced on May 5 that it would cut 700 jobs, or roughly 14% of its workforce, as it shifts toward a more AI-centric workflow that involves deploying agents

stories.headline: US government will now evaluate Google DeepMind, Microsoft, and xAI models before public release under new NIST agreements
stories.summary: The Center for AI Standards and Innovation, housed inside NIST, announced new agreements with Google DeepMind, Microsoft, and xAI allowing the US government to evaluate AI models before they are publicly released, assess them post-deployment, and pursue targeted research on frontier AI security risks. The agreements expand a federal testing program tied to the Trump administration's AI policy.
stories.raw_excerpt: New agreements with Google DeepMind, Microsoft and xAI will let the U.S. government evaluate models before they are publicly released

stories.headline: AI data centres will soon consume as much electricity as two-thirds of all American homes, BCG report finds
stories.summary: A Boston Consulting Group report released May 4 projected that AI data centres alone will consume the same electricity currently used by roughly two-thirds of all US homes by 2030. The report follows executive orders to accelerate nuclear energy deployment, as nuclear is identified as the only energy source capable of meeting AI data centre baseload requirements at scale. Separately, 30-50% of planned 2026 data centre capacity is projected to slip to 2028 due to grid and supply chain bottlenecks.
stories.raw_excerpt: By 2030, AI data centers alone will consume the same amount of electricity currently used by roughly two-thirds of all homes in the United States

stories.headline: NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang admits China AI accelerator market share has dropped to zero as Huawei fills the gap
stories.summary: NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang disclosed that the company's AI accelerator market share in China has effectively dropped to zero following US export restrictions. Huawei and domestic Chinese companies such as Cambricon are increasingly filling the gap. Huang made the comments while separately arguing that AI is creating, rather than destroying, jobs overall.
stories.raw_excerpt: NVIDIA's AI accelerator market share in China has dropped to zero, as local companies such as Huawei and Cambricon are increasingly filling the gap

stories.headline: Colorado pushes back start date of its landmark 2024 AI law to January 2027, narrowing its scope
stories.summary: A new Colorado bill (SB 189) pushed back the effective date of Colorado's landmark 2024 AI law from June 2026 to January 2027 and narrowed its scope. A separate bill dropped the requirement that companies explain how their AI technology works, representing a significant rollback of the original transparency provisions.
stories.raw_excerpt: Senate Bill 189 pushes back the start date of Colorado's AI law regulating AI to January 2027 from June

stories.headline: Microsoft launches Agent 365 as its governance and security control plane for all AI agents across Microsoft 365
stories.summary: Microsoft announced Agent 365 is generally available, positioning AI agents as the next operating layer across Microsoft 365. The platform includes a registry that discovers, inventories, and performs lifecycle governance for agents across AWS and Google Cloud connections. Starting June 2026, Microsoft Defender will provide asset context mapping for each agent. Microsoft research claims 78% of knowledge workers now use AI agents weekly, up from 12% in 2024.
stories.raw_excerpt: 78% of knowledge workers now use AI agents at least weekly, up from just 12% in 2024

stories.headline: Microsoft removes Copilot AI assistant from Xbox entirely
stories.summary: Microsoft announced it would completely remove the Copilot AI assistant from Xbox. No specific reason was given publicly. The removal is notable given Microsoft's aggressive Copilot integration elsewhere and raises questions about where AI assistants are actually being adopted versus where they are proving useless to users.
stories.raw_excerpt: Microsoft kicks Copilot out of Xbox

stories.headline: EU AI Act GPAI enforcement powers take effect August 2, 2026 — major compliance deadline approaching
stories.summary: The remaining provisions of the EU AI Act will become applicable on August 2, 2026, including the Commission's enforcement powers over general-purpose AI model providers. The Commission will be able to request documentation, conduct evaluations, request compliance measures, and impose fines. Member states must also establish AI regulatory sandboxes by this date. This represents the most significant AI regulatory enforcement deadline since GDPR.
stories.raw_excerpt: On 2 August 2026, the Commission's enforcement powers in respect of GPAI model providers will come into force

stories.headline: Big Tech's $725 billion AI spending spree is being funded by mass layoffs — 113,000 workers cut in 2026 so far
stories.summary: As of May 6, 2026, there have been 179 separate layoff events impacting 113,863 workers in the tech sector this year, averaging approximately 904 job losses per day. Invezz and the Washington Post both published analyses questioning whether AI is genuinely driving the layoffs or whether they are cover for broader austerity while AI capex surges.
stories.raw_excerpt: As of May 6, 2026, there have been 179 layoff events in 2026, impacting 113,863 workers

stories.headline: Four Chinese AI labs release competitive open-weights coding models within 12 days, matching Western frontier at a fraction of the cost
stories.summary: Four Chinese AI labs — Z.ai (GLM-5.1), MiniMax (M2.7), Moonshot (Kimi K2.6), and DeepSeek (V4) — released open-weights coding models within a 12-day window in late April/early May 2026. All four reached roughly the same capability ceiling on agentic engineering tasks as Western frontier models at meaningfully lower inference cost. DeepSeek V4 is a 1.6-trillion-parameter model built on zero Nvidia hardware.
stories.raw_excerpt: Four Chinese labs released open-weights coding models inside a 12-day window at roughly the same capability ceiling on agentic engineering at meaningfully lower inference cost than the Western frontier
